summary: libSkiaSharp.so in SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Android was built with 4 KB memory page alignment instead of the 16 KB alignment required by Google Play for Android 16+ 64-bit libraries; the fix (-Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384) was included in 3.118.0.

bugSignals.errorMessage: libSkiaSharp.so shows 4 KB page size; Google Play requires 16 KB for Android 16+ 64-bit libs

stepsToReproduce: Create a .NET MAUI Android app targeting Android 16
Build AAB using SkiaSharp 3.116.0 / MAUI workload
Run page size check on .so files
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Android v3.116.0 (described as 2.88.6 in body but 3.116.0 in version field), .NET MAUI 9.0, Android 16+

repoLinks.description: Google official guide on 16 KB page size requirement
MAUI community discussion on the same requirement
Related issue: libSkiaSharp.so alignment warning, closed as completed in milestone 3.118.0

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The linker flag -Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384 is already present in native/android/build.cake (line 42), meaning the fix was shipped in 3.118.0. Reporter used 3.116.0 which predates the fix.

fixStatus.reason: native/android/build.cake already contains extra_ldflags=[ '-Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384' ] ensuring 16 KB alignment. Related issue #3025 was closed as 'completed' in milestone 3.118.0.

analysis.summary: The Android native build script now passes -Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384 to the linker (native/android/build.cake:42), which forces ELF segment alignment to 16 KB. This fix was part of milestone 3.118.0, closing related issue #3025. The reporter was on 3.116.0, which predates the fix; upgrading to 3.118.0 or later resolves the compliance issue.

codeInvestigation.finding: extra_ldflags contains '-Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384' — this linker flag sets ELF load segment alignment to 16 KB, satisfying Google Play's Android 16+ requirement for 64-bit native libraries.
The libSkiaSharp GN/Ninja build target passes the 16 KB page-size flag to all Android architectures (x86, x86_64, armeabi-v7a, arm64-v8a), meaning the fix is broad and not architecture-gated.

keySignals.text: libSkiaSharp.so included in SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Android v2.88.6 has a 4 KB page size
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter identifies the root cause as the linker alignment setting at build time.

keySignals.text: Google Play now requires all 64-bit native libraries to use 16 KB memory page size starting with Android 16+
keySignals.interpretation: This is a Google Play Store compliance requirement, not a runtime crash — it blocks publishing.
Fix is already in the current codebase. The 16 KB alignment flag was added before this issue was filed (shipped in 3.118.0).

keySignals.text: Have you tried the 3.x series of SkiaSharp?
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer hinting the fix may be present in a newer 3.x release.

analysis.rationale: This is a native build compliance bug: the Android shared library was linked without the 16 KB page alignment flag required by Google Play for Android 16+ 64-bit apps. Code investigation confirms the fix (-Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384) is present in the current build script. Related issue #3025 (same alignment problem) was closed as completed in 3.118.0. The reporter was on 3.116.0, so upgrading should resolve it. Suggested action is close-as-fixed with a comment pointing to 3.118.0+.
workarounds: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.118.0 or later — the 16 KB page-size linker flag is included.
If stuck on 2.88.x, manually recompile the Android native library with -Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384 (complex, not recommended).

resolution.hypothesis: The fix (max-page-size=16384 linker flag) was shipped in 3.118.0 in response to issue #3025, which described the same alignment problem. The reporter needs to upgrade from 3.116.0 to 3.118.0 or later.

proposals.title: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.118.0 or later
proposals.description: Upgrade the SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Android package to 3.118.0 or newer. The linker flag -Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384 was added to native/android/build.cake in that release, ensuring Google Play Android 16+ compliance.

resolution.recommendedReason: Zero-effort fix — just a package version bump. The underlying code change is already in the current release.

actionability.reason: The 16 KB page-size linker flag is present in the current native/android/build.cake. Issue #3025 (same root cause) was closed as completed in milestone 3.118.0. Reporter needs to upgrade from 3.116.0.

actions.description: Labels already correctly applied; confirm existing set
Inform reporter that the fix shipped in 3.118.0 and they should upgrade
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed report! This issue was resolved in **SkiaSharp 3.118.0** — the Android native library is now built with the `-Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384` linker flag, which sets ELF segment alignment to 16 KB to satisfy Google Play's Android 16+ requirement.

Please upgrade your `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Android` (and related SkiaSharp packages) to **3.118.0 or later** and the warning/error should disappear.

This was also tracked in #3025 which was closed as completed in 3.118.0.

If you're still seeing the issue after upgrading to 3.118.0+, please reopen with details.

actions.description: Cross-reference the earlier related issue that was closed as completed
Close as fixed — the fix was shipped in 3.118.0
